headline: Starbucks Has Its Shine Back, CEO Brian Niccol Says
summary: More than a year into Starbucks’s ambitious turnaround plan, Chief Executive Brian Niccol said the world’s largest coffee chain is getting its groove back.  Niccol said during a Wednesday shareholder call that investments in barista staffing, paired with a surge in new for-here service coffeehouses, is helping Starbucks regain its former luster.  The Seattle-based chain expects to build more than 1,000 of these coffeehouses by the end of the fiscal year, ramping toward its goal of 5,000 in the U.S.

headline: BROS' Record AUVs Stand Out: Can Unit Economics Stay This Strong?
summary: BROS hits record $2.1M AUVs on strong traffic growth, but as expansion accelerates, can it sustain the standout unit economics?

headline: Can Starbucks' $2B Cost-Savings Plan Reignite Margin Expansion?
summary: SBUX targets $2B in cost savings over two years, aiming to boost efficiency and support margin recovery amid ongoing investments.

headline: Starbucks tests new ordering channels for licensed stores
summary: Kiosks and scheduled orders could be coming to Starbucks cafes in airports, as the chain looks to speed up operations at high-traffic licensed stores.
